ux_issues.description: In step 3b (Oaxaca planning), the AI response contained the expert greeting repeated 3 times verbatim ('From the mole in Oaxaca to the tequila in Jalisco, let's taste the heart of Mexico together') along with repeated paragraphs about CDMX vs Oaxacan food culture. This appears to be a tool-loop repetition issue where the AI re-emits its preamble each time it calls create_itinerary_items.
ux_issues.suggestion: Investigate why the AI re-emits its greeting and intro text on each tool loop iteration. The tool loop should only produce the continuation text, not re-state the entire opening.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: recommend_booking not triggered on first cooking class request
ai_behavior_issues.context: When asked 'Can you recommend some cooking classes and food tours I should book in Oaxaca? I want real hands-on mole classes and market tours. Looking for mid-range prices, maybe $80-150 per person', the AI created itinerary items and used delete_itinerary_items but did NOT call recommend_booking. Only a second, more explicit request ('find me bookable activities... show me options with prices and booking links') triggered recommend_booking. The first phrasing should have been sufficient to trigger affiliate booking recommendations.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI performed well overall as a food travel companion. Trip creation correctly captured the food/culinary focus. The expert persona (Mateo) demonstrated genuine depth on Mexican food culture, correctly distinguishing CDMX cosmopolitan food from Oaxacan traditional cuisine. The itinerary included specific palenque recommendations (Real Minero, Vago Espiritus), multiple agave varieties (Tobala, Tepeztate, Arroqueno), Dia de Muertos food traditions (pan de muerto, calaveras de azucar, nicuatole), practical details like market neighborhoods and reservation warnings for Pujol/Quintonil, and appropriate budget-level recommendations ($80-150 cooking classes). Booking ingestion parsed all tour details flawlessly. Companion mode was concise and practical with peso-denominated tips and specific transport advice. The main weakness was repetitive text in multi-tool-call responses and recommend_booking requiring an overly explicit prompt to trigger.
